chapter_context.summary: David addresses the national assembly, recalls God's choice of Solomon to build the Temple, charges Solomon to know God, and hands him the divinely inspired blueprints.

outline.text: David Addresses Assembly; Recalls God's Choice of Solomon
outline.explanation: This section focuses on David Addresses Assembly; Recalls God's Choice of Solomon (28:1-8), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Chronicles.

outline.text: David's Personal Charge to Solomon: 'Know the God of thy father'
outline.explanation: This section focuses on David's Personal Charge to Solomon: 'Know the God of thy father' (28:9-10), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Chronicles.

outline.text: David Hands Over the Inspired Blueprints and Gold Weight Specifications
outline.explanation: This section focuses on David Hands Over the Inspired Blueprints and Gold Weight Specifications (28:11-21), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Chronicles.

key_themes.title: Covenant Lineage
key_themes.description: The meticulous genealogies show that God's plan is continuous and links post-exilic Israel back to creation.

key_themes.title: The Throne of David
key_themes.description: David's rise and worship preparations focus on establishing Jerusalem as the central theological hub of the kingdom.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the events of Chapter 28, how does seeing God's faithfulness through history encourage you today?
How does this chapter prompt you to dedicate your personal plans and gifts to the LORD's work?

cultural_historical.title: The Post-Exilic Restoration
cultural_historical.text: Chronicles was written for the returning exiles to rebuild their religious identity, focusing heavily on priestly lineages and temple worship.
